source_jobs.title: Project Manager
source_jobs.description:
TITLE: PROJECT MANAGER / SENIOR PROJECT MANAGER
DEPARTMENT: ENGINEERING – WATER UTILITY INFRASTRUCTURE
LOCATION: INDIANAPOLIS, IN
EMPLOYMENT TYPE: FULL-TIME

OVERVIEW

Our client is seeking a motivated Project Manager to oversee utility infrastructure projects from planning through delivery. This role balances technical oversight, client coordination, and project execution, with opportunities for professional growth and mentorship under senior leadership.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage utility infrastructure projects including drinking water, wastewater, and stormwater systems.

 - Develop project scopes, schedules, budgets, and proposals.

 - Coordinate with clients, subconsultants, regulatory agencies, and internal teams.

 - Supervise project staff and mentor engineers/technicians under senior guidance.

 - Perform QA/QC reviews, address design issues, and assist with construction-related problems.

 - Track labor utilization, revenue forecasting, billing, and documentation.

 - Conduct occasional site visits and construction observations.



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Registered Professional Engineer (PE).

 - 7+ years of experience in utility infrastructure engineering.

 - Proficiency in project management and client communication.

 - Experience delivering projects on time, within budget, and meeting quality standards.

 - Knowledge of utility design standards, regulations, and construction practices.



WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE

 - Take ownership of meaningful infrastructure projects.

 - Collaborate with a dynamic team while gaining mentorship from senior leaders.

 - Competitive benefits and career advancement opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Project Manager - Forensic Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Overview:
Join a dynamic, values-driven engineering and architecture firm dedicated to delivering exceptional service to clients and communities. Our client emphasizes respect, professional growth, collaboration, and results. Team members are encouraged to explore innovative solutions, contribute to meaningful projects, and help turn clients’ visions into reality. With competitive benefits, training, and mentorship, this role offers a pathway to a rewarding career beyond just a job.



Position Summary:
We are seeking a Project Manager with experience in forensic engineering or architecture to lead and support a variety of investigative projects. The role involves assessing structural and building enclosure conditions, performing damage and deterioration investigations, and designing repair and rehabilitation solutions for existing facilities. Projects span commercial, residential, industrial, utility infrastructure, and municipal sectors.

This position requires strong technical expertise, excellent communication skills, and the ability to manage multiple assignments while interacting directly with clients and staff. Frequent local and occasional regional travel is required.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Conduct investigations and assessments, preparing accurate technical reports and repair documentation under guidance of senior staff.

 - Manage multiple projects simultaneously and support project scheduling and coordination.

 - Assist in developing repair designs, drawings, and technical specifications.

 - Support senior staff with technical research, report writing, and project execution.

 - Mentor and guide Staff Engineers and Interns as needed.

 - Perform other duties as assigned.



Physical Requirements:

 - Ability to lift or move objects up to 50 lbs.

 - Willingness to climb ladders and scaffolding, access crawl spaces, walk roofs, and work from heights.

 - Comfortable working outdoors in various weather conditions.



Qualifications:

 - Bachelor’s Degree in Civil or Structural Engineering, Architecture, or related field; other degrees considered with relevant experience.

 - Engineering Intern certification or passed NCEES FE exam preferred.

 - Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience in engineering, architecture, or forensic investigations.

 - Experience with AutoCAD and/or Revit is a plus.

 - Prior design and/or forensic investigation experience preferred.

 - Proficiency in MS Office applications.

 - Valid driver’s license and reliable personal transportation.

 - Strong oral and written communication skills.

Why Apply:
This role offers the opportunity to work on challenging projects in a collaborative environment, with professional development and mentorship opportunities. If you have a passion for forensic investigations and structural problem-solving, and want to contribute to meaningful projects, this position is for you.

source_jobs.title: IT Consultant
source_jobs.description:
Location: Indianapolis, IN (On-site)
Type: Full-Time
Industry: Civil Engineering / Infrastructure




OVERVIEW

Our client is seeking an experienced IT Consultant to join their growing IT Team. This role is ideal for a polished, service-oriented professional who excels at translating technical complexity into clear, actionable guidance for clients. You will work directly with business leaders and technical teams to design, implement, and support infrastructure solutions that drive meaningful business outcomes. Candidates who thrive in client-facing environments, enjoy problem solving, and bring a consultative mindset will excel in this position.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Engage directly with clients to understand business and technical requirements, ensuring solutions align with organizational objectives.

 - Provide strategic guidance on technology roadmaps, infrastructure optimization, and best-practice implementations.

 - Lead and support IT initiatives while coordinating with internal technical teams to deliver high-quality results.

 - Design and implement infrastructure solutions following industry standards and structured methodologies.

 - Evaluate existing environments and recommend improvements to performance, reliability, and security.

 - Build strong, trust-based relationships through clear communication and proactive support.

 - Collaborate with peers to drive consistent, high-quality outcomes across all client engagements.

 - Perform additional duties as needed to support client and team success.



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience.

 - Experience supporting large or enterprise-level IT environments.

 - Minimum 4 years of hands-on experience with Microsoft Windows Server and VMware technologies.

 - Certifications a plus, including:
   
   - MCSA
   
   - Microsoft Cloud (Fundamental or advanced)
   
   - VCP
   
   - CCNP or CCNA
   
   - Security certifications

 - Background in systems or infrastructure administration or IT leadership is beneficial.

 - Strong analytical approach to troubleshooting and solution design.

 - Detail-oriented with the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively.

 - Passion for continuous learning and the ability to adopt new technologies

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present ideas clearly and objectively.



WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE

 - You will make a direct impact by helping clients strategically leverage technology to solve business challenges.

 - You’ll work in a collaborative, client-focused environment that values strong communication, professionalism, and high-quality delivery.

 - The role offers exposure to diverse infrastructure projects and the opportunity to deepen your expertise across modern technologies.

 - You’ll engage in meaningful face-to-face interactions with clients and teammates, building long-term relationships and trust.

 - A growth-minded environment encourages continuous learning, certifications, and skill expansion.

source_jobs.title: Agency Recruiter - Full Desk
source_jobs.description:
AGENCY RECRUITER – FULL DESK (INDEPENDENT / 1099)

Location: Remote (U.S.)
Model: Independent Contractor / 100% Commission






This opportunity is built by and for experienced full-desk recruiters. We are a collective of proven professionals who have run their own desks, managed client relationships end-to-end, and understand what it takes to generate revenue.

We are not a traditional agency—and we are not a place to “learn recruiting.” This is a peer-level environment for recruiters who value ownership, flexibility, and independence, while recognizing that collaboration can accelerate growth.

You keep control of your clients, searches, and workflow while gaining access to shared systems, modern recruiting technology, and a trusted network of fellow producers.




THE ROLE

As a Senior Recruiter in our network, you will operate your own full desk—business development, client management, and delivery—while having the option to collaborate with other experienced recruiters when it makes sense.

This model is designed for recruiters who are already successful but want to scale smarter, reduce overhead, and avoid working in isolation.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Run a full recruiting desk, including client acquisition, relationship management, and candidate delivery

 - Own your client relationships and manage searches from intake through placement

 - Collaborate with other senior recruiters on shared searches, role-splits, or candidate pipelines

 - Exchange market insights, sourcing strategies, and best practices with peer recruiters

 - Leverage shared recruiting technology, ATS, and AI-driven tools without building infrastructure alone

 - Contribute strategically to a growing recruiter network built on trust and performance


WHY EXPERIENCED RECRUITERS JOIN

 - True Desk Ownership: You control your clients, candidates, and approach—no hand-holding, no micromanagement

 - Peer-Level Collaboration: Work with other proven full-desk recruiters, not trainees or junior staff

 - Scalable Infrastructure: Access enterprise-level recruiting systems, AI tools, and processes without agency overhead

 - Flexible Independence: Run your business your way while staying connected to a strong professional network

 - Expanded Earning Potential: Collaborate on larger or more complex searches and unlock shared revenue opportunities


WHO THIS IS FOR

 - Experienced full-desk agency recruiters

 - Independent recruiters with a track record of placements

 - Professionals who can self-manage, self-source, and self-close

 - Recruiters who value autonomy but see the advantage of collaboration

 - High performers seeking uncapped earning potential without a traditional agency model


COMPENSATION

This is a 100% commission-based, 1099 role. Recruiters earn a competitive share of placement fees, paid once client invoices are collected. There are no caps—performance directly drives income.

source_jobs.title: Team Leader - Water Utility Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
BUILD AND LEAD A WATER UTILITY INFRASTRUCTURE PRACTICE — DALLAS, TX


We’re partnering with a highly respected engineering firm to identify a senior leader who’s ready to build, lead, and grow a water utility infrastructure office in Dallas. This is a rare opportunity to step into a true ownership-minded role—shaping strategy, developing talent, and expanding a high-impact practice serving municipal and utility clients across North Texas.


As the office leader and team leader, you’ll oversee operations, mentor and grow a multidisciplinary team, serve as a technical authority, and act as a trusted advisor to clients. If you’re energized by building teams, elevating people, and delivering complex infrastructure projects, this role offers both influence and long-term growth.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN


PRACTICE & TEAM LEADERSHIP

 - Lead day-to-day operations for the Dallas water utility infrastructure practice

 - Oversee workflow, staffing plans, utilization, and profitability

 - Prepare monthly staffing and financial forecasts with a forward-looking outlook

 - Maintain visibility into project performance, billings, and margins

 - Partner with senior leadership to align resources and drive growth

 - Identify hiring needs and play a key role in recruiting top engineering talent

 - Foster a high-performance, collaborative, and engaged team culture

 - Lead performance reviews and support employee development and retention


STAFF DEVELOPMENT & MENTORSHIP

 - Actively mentor Engineers and Project Managers at all career stages

 - Build technical depth and leadership capability across the team

 - Develop Project Managers into well-rounded leaders of scope, schedule, budget, and client relationships

 - Coach PMs on proposals, fee development, billing, and financial performance

 - Create and track individualized development plans and progression timelines


TECHNICAL LEADERSHIP

 - Provide technical leadership across drinking water, wastewater, and stormwater projects

 - Lead and oversee studies, designs, and complex utility infrastructure solutions

 - Anticipate and resolve technical challenges to keep projects moving efficiently

 - Serve as a go-to technical resource for both staff and clients


PROJECT & FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT

 - Lead proposal development, fee negotiations, and contract execution

 - Monitor project financial performance and guide PMs toward consistent delivery

 - Ensure schedules, budgets, and quality standards are met across all projects

 - Contribute to overall group and cross-discipline profitability


QUALITY & CLIENT ENGAGEMENT

 - Ensure rigorous technical review and quality control at all project stages

 - Leverage firmwide expertise to deliver best-in-class solutions

 - Build long-term, trusted relationships with municipal and utility clients

 - Identify expansion opportunities with existing clients and support new pursuits

 - Partner with business development and sales teams to grow the practice



WHAT OUR CLIENT IS LOOKING FOR


EDUCATION & CREDENTIALS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Civil or Environmental Engineering

 - REQUIRED: Professional Engineer (PE) license in Texas


EXPERIENCE

 - 10+ years of utility infrastructure engineering experience

 - Strong background in water and wastewater treatment facilities, distribution and collection systems, and lift stations

 - Proven history of high client satisfaction and repeat work


LEADERSHIP & BUSINESS ACUMEN

 - Confident, positive communicator at all organizational levels

 - Demonstrated success leading, mentoring, and developing teams

 - Experience supporting proposals, presentations, and winning new work

 - Ability to contribute to business planning, growth strategy, and practice expansion


TECHNICAL & MARKET PRESENCE

 - Strong track record of profitable project delivery

 - Established relationships with local municipalities and utility clients



WHY THIS OPPORTUNITY STANDS OUT

 - Build your own team and shape the Dallas office’s fut

source_jobs.title: IT Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Location: Indianapolis, IN (On-site)
Type: Full-Time
Industry: Civil Engineering / Infrastructure



OVERVIEW

Our client is looking for a hands-on Systems Engineer who wants to work on real, enterprise-scale infrastructure and actually see the impact of their work. This role sits at the core of our IT environment, supporting critical systems, modern endpoint management, virtualization, and security.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Windows Server environments, including Active Directory, DNS, DHCP, and Group Policy - Administration, configuration and maintain.

 - VMware ESXi/vSphere cluster deployment- Management and optimization

 - Citrix infrastructure, including XenApp/XenDesktop, Citrix Virtual Apps and Desktops, and NetScaler (ADC) appliance - Maintain and troubleshoot.

 - Ivanti Endpoint Management - Administration for asset inventory, patch management, software deployment, OS deployment, and remote support, ensuring compliance with corporate security policies. Including design, test, and implement patches.

 - Microsoft Intune for mobile device management (MDM) and mobile application management (MAM) - Manage while supporting policy configuration, device enrollment, compliance monitoring, and application deployment for iOS, Android, and Windows devices.

 - Oversee enterprise imaging technologies.

 - Monitor system performance, capacity, and availability.

 - Implement and maintain security best practices, including system hardening, vulnerability remediation, and endpoint security posture management.

 - Tier 3/4 technical support and mentoring.

 - Disaster recovery participation.

 - Infrastructure design, architecture decisions, and technical documentation.



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent experience.

 - 5 or more years of experience in systems engineering or infrastructure administration in a mid to large enterprise.

 - Strong customer service, communication, and problem-solving skills.

 - Ability to work independently or with a team in enterprise environment.

 - Tech Stack:
   
   - VMware ESXi/vSphere configuration, migration, and troubleshooting strengths.
   
   - Citrix Virtual Apps and Desktops and related technologies experience.
   
   - Cisco networking and Check Point firewall management understanding
   
   - Ivanti Endpoint Management, including OS deployment, software distribution, and automated patch management experience.
   
   - Microsoft Intune and mobile device management platforms experience.
   
   - Enterprise monitoring and logging tools (e.g., SolarWinds, etc.) experience.

 - Nice to Have:
   
   - Certifications such as MCSE, VCP, CCA-V or CCP-V, or Microsoft Endpoint Administrator.
   
   - Cloud platform experience (MS Azure ideal).
   
   - Microsoft 365 experiences.
   
   - Scripting (PowerShell, Python).
   
   - Backup and recovery solutions (Rubrik).




WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE

 - You will be part of an internal team that values learning, collaboration, and quick problem solving.

 - You will help people directly and see the impact of your work every day.

 - Solve complex problems, and help shape infrastructure.

 - You will work in an environment that appreciates initiative, ownership, and follow-through.

source_jobs.title: Senior Project Engineer
source_jobs.description:
TITLE: PROJECT ENGINEER / SENIOR PROJECT ENGINEER
DEPARTMENT: ENGINEERING – WATER UTILITY INFRASTRUCTURE
LOCATION: AKRON, OH
EMPLOYMENT TYPE: FULL-TIME

WORK ARRANGEMENT: ON-SITE WITH TRAVEL AND FIELD WORK
COMPENSATION: $70K – $130K (COMMENSURATE WITH EXPERIENCE)
+ BONUS + BENEFITS
OVERVIEW

Join a growing Utility Infrastructure team delivering impactful water, wastewater, and stormwater projects that directly support public health and sustainable communities. This role is designed for engineers at varying experience levels, from developing professionals building technical depth to seasoned engineers leading complex design efforts.

You will contribute to projects from concept through construction, collaborating with Project Managers and senior technical staff while gaining increasing ownership based on your experience level.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design and deliver water, wastewater, and stormwater infrastructure projects, including distribution systems, pumping stations, treatment facilities, green infrastructure, and utility relocations

 - Prepare and review design drawings, specifications, calculations, permitting documents, and construction exhibits

 - Perform engineering calculations, technical evaluations, and product research (increasing in complexity with experience)

 - Interpret and apply regulatory requirements to ensure compliant, constructible designs

 - Identify design challenges and develop practical, efficient, and innovative solutions

 - Collaborate with Project Managers on technical execution, planning, and problem-solving

 - Support construction administration through RFIs, submittals, site visits, and field reports

 - Coordinate with internal teams, clients, and external partners for seamless project delivery

 - Contribute to quality assurance through review of engineering documents and deliverables

 - Mentor and support junior staff (senior-level expectation; developing skill at PE level)

 - Serve as technical representative of firm at all required project meetings, presentations, and public hearings




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Environmental Engineering or related field

 - Professional Engineer (PE) license required

 - Experience range:
   
   - Project Engineer: ~4+ years of relevant experience
   
   - Senior Project Engineer: 10+ years of diversified experience

 - Background in water, wastewater, stormwater, and municipal or public utility projects

 - Strong understanding of regulatory requirements, engineering standards, and infrastructure best practices

 - Proven ability to handle increasing technical complexity and project responsibility with experience

 - Active involvement or interest in professional organizations (e.g., WEF, AWWA, ASCE)

 - Travel: Occasional day trips to project sites and client meetings

 - Field Work: Occasional site assessment, field measurements, construction observation




WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE

Purpose-driven work: Design infrastructure that directly improves public health, sustainability, and quality of life

 - Growth and mentorship: Learn from senior engineers while building leadership and technical expertise

 - Ownership and impact: Take real responsibility for projects, not just pieces of them

 - Collaborative culture: Work with cross-functional teams that value communication and shared success

 - Variety and challenge: No two projects are the same, keeping your work engaging and meaningful

 - Career momentum: Clear opportunities to advance technically and professionally

source_jobs.title: Senior Project Manager
source_jobs.description:
TITLE: PROJECT MANAGER / SENIOR PROJECT MANAGER
DEPARTMENT: ENGINEERING – WATER UTILITY INFRASTRUCTURE
LOCATION: INDIANAPOLIS, IN
EMPLOYMENT TYPE: FULL-TIME

COMPENSATION: $100K – $145K (COMMENSURATE WITH EXPERIENCE)
+ BONUS + BENEFITS

OVERVIEW

Our client is seeking a Project Manager or Senior Project Manager to lead utility infrastructure projects from planning through delivery. This role supports a wide range of water, wastewater, and stormwater initiatives, with responsibilities scaled based on experience. You will collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, engage directly with clients, and play a key role in delivering high-quality infrastructure solutions that serve communities. Senior-level candidates will take on broader leadership, mentoring, and strategic responsibilities..





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage and deliver utility infrastructure projects, including drinking water, wastewater, and stormwater systems.

 - Develop project scopes, schedules, budgets, and proposals.

 - Coordinate with clients, subconsultants, regulatory agencies, and internal teams.

 - Monitor project performance, including budget, schedule, quality, and risk.

 - Ensure QA/QC compliance for all project deliverables and documentation.

 - Track labor utilization, revenue forecasting, billing, and documentation.

 - Serve as the firm’s representative at client meetings, public hearings, and presentations.

 - Conduct site visits and construction observations as needed.

 - Lead multiple utility infrastructure projects from concept to completion, ensuring schedule, budget, and quality targets are met.

 - Mentor engineers and project managers, providing technical guidance and career development support. Oversee proposals, contracts, fee justifications, and budget forecasting.



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Registered Professional Engineer (PE).

 - Proven track record managing drinking water, wastewater, and stormwater projects.

 - Experience range:
   
   - Project Manager: 7+ years of relevant experience
   
   - Senior Project Manager: 10+ years of relevant experience

 - Experience delivering projects on time, within budget, and meeting quality standards.

 - Knowledge of utility design standards, regulations, and construction practices.

 - Excellent communication, leadership, and client management skills.



WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE

 - Take ownership of meaningful infrastructure projects.

 - Collaborate with a dynamic team while gaining mentorship from senior leaders.

 - Competitive benefits and career advancement opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Senior Project Manager - Forensic Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Location: Orlando, FL (onsite)
Type: Full-Time
Industry: Forensic Civil Engineering / Infrastructure

Overview:
Join a dynamic, values-driven engineering and architecture firm dedicated to delivering exceptional service to clients and communities. Our client emphasizes respect, professional growth, collaboration, and results. Team members are encouraged to explore innovative solutions, contribute to meaningful projects, and help turn clients’ visions into reality. With competitive benefits, training, and mentorship, this role offers a pathway to a rewarding career beyond just a job.



Position Summary:
We are seeking a Senior Project Manager with experience in forensic engineering or architecture to lead and support a variety of investigative projects. The role involves assessing structural and building enclosure conditions, performing damage and deterioration investigations, and designing repair and rehabilitation solutions for existing facilities. Projects span commercial, residential, industrial, utility infrastructure, and municipal sectors.

This position requires strong technical expertise, excellent communication skills, and the ability to manage multiple assignments while interacting directly with clients and staff. Frequent local and occasional regional travel is required.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Conduct investigations and assessments, preparing accurate technical reports and repair documentation under guidance of senior staff.

 - Manage multiple projects simultaneously and support project scheduling and coordination.

 - Assist in developing repair designs, drawings, and technical specifications.

 - Support senior staff with technical research, report writing, and project execution.

 - Mentor and guide Staff Engineers and Interns as needed.

 - Perform other duties as assigned.



Physical Requirements:

 - Ability to lift or move objects up to 50 lbs.

 - Willingness to climb ladders and scaffolding, access crawl spaces, walk roofs, and work from heights.

 - Comfortable working outdoors in various weather conditions.



Qualifications:

 - Minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree in Civil or Structural Engineering or Architecture from an accredited engineering or architecture school; Other degrees will be considered based on relevant experience

 - Registered Professional Engineer, NCARB Registered Architect, or similar industry licensure/certification

 - 10+ years’ experience

 - AutoCAD and/or Revit experience is a plus

 - Prior design and/or investigation experience preferred

 - Proficient with MS Office applications

 - Valid driver’s license and reliable means of personal transportation

Why Apply:
This role offers the opportunity to work on challenging projects in a collaborative environment, with professional development and mentorship opportunities. If you have a passion for forensic investigations and structural problem-solving, and want to contribute to meaningful projects, this position is for you.

 - Proficient with MS Office applications

 - Valid driver’s license and reliable means of personal transportation

Why Apply:
This role offers the opportunity to work on challenging projects in a collaborative environment, with professional development and mentorship opportunities. If you have a passion for forensic investigations and structural problem-solving, and want to contribute to meaningful projects, this position is for you.

source_jobs.title: Senior Project Engineer
source_jobs.description:
TITLE: PROJECT ENGINEER / SENIOR PROJECT ENGINEER
DEPARTMENT: ENGINEERING – WATER UTILITY INFRASTRUCTURE
LOCATION: CLEVELAND, OH
EMPLOYMENT TYPE: FULL-TIME

WORK ARRANGEMENT: ON-SITE WITH TRAVEL AND FIELD WORK
COMPENSATION: $70K – $130K (COMMENSURATE WITH EXPERIENCE)
+ BONUS + BENEFITS
OVERVIEW

Join a growing Utility Infrastructure team delivering impactful water, wastewater, and stormwater projects that directly support public health and sustainable communities. This role is designed for engineers at varying experience levels, from developing professionals building technical depth to seasoned engineers leading complex design efforts.

You will contribute to projects from concept through construction, collaborating with Project Managers and senior technical staff while gaining increasing ownership based on your experience level.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design and deliver water, wastewater, and stormwater infrastructure projects, including distribution systems, pumping stations, treatment facilities, green infrastructure, and utility relocations

 - Prepare and review design drawings, specifications, calculations, permitting documents, and construction exhibits

 - Perform engineering calculations, technical evaluations, and product research (increasing in complexity with experience)

 - Interpret and apply regulatory requirements to ensure compliant, constructible designs

 - Identify design challenges and develop practical, efficient, and innovative solutions

 - Collaborate with Project Managers on technical execution, planning, and problem-solving

 - Support construction administration through RFIs, submittals, site visits, and field reports

 - Coordinate with internal teams, clients, and external partners for seamless project delivery

 - Contribute to quality assurance through review of engineering documents and deliverables

 - Mentor and support junior staff (senior-level expectation; developing skill at PE level)

 - Serve as technical representative of firm at all required project meetings, presentations, and public hearings




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Environmental Engineering or related field

 - Professional Engineer (PE) license required

 - Experience range:
   
   - Project Engineer: ~4+ years of relevant experience
   
   - Senior Project Engineer: 10+ years of diversified experience

 - Background in water, wastewater, stormwater, and municipal or public utility projects

 - Strong understanding of regulatory requirements, engineering standards, and infrastructure best practices

 - Proven ability to handle increasing technical complexity and project responsibility with experience

 - Active involvement or interest in professional organizations (e.g., WEF, AWWA, ASCE)

 - Travel: Occasional day trips to project sites and client meetings

 - Field Work: Occasional site assessment, field measurements, construction observation




WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE

Purpose-driven work: Design infrastructure that directly improves public health, sustainability, and quality of life

 - Growth and mentorship: Learn from senior engineers while building leadership and technical expertise

 - Ownership and impact: Take real responsibility for projects, not just pieces of them

 - Collaborative culture: Work with cross-functional teams that value communication and shared success

 - Variety and challenge: No two projects are the same, keeping your work engaging and meaningful

 - Career momentum: Clear opportunities to advance technically and professionally

source_jobs.title: Senior Applied Scientist
source_jobs.description:
Location: Seattle, WA (On-site downtown) - Hybrid
Type: Full-Time
Industry: Software (AI)
Specialty: Identity verification, fraud prevention, background screening, and risk intelligence.



OVERVIEW

Our publicly traded client develops proprietary technology and analytics to deliver identity intelligence, powering critical solutions that help organizations operate confidently. Their solutions enable real-time identification and location of people, businesses, assets, and their relationships for risk mitigation, due diligence, fraud prevention, regulatory compliance, and customer acquisition. These solutions support frictionless commerce, enhance safety, reduce fraud, and lower related societal costs.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Our client is looking for a collaborative and forward-thinking Senior Applied Scientist to join their Seattle-based AI team. In this role, your work will focus on developing and deploying generative AI, agentic AI, and deep learning-based solutions.

You’ll partner with a diverse team of scientists, engineers, and product leaders to build scalable solutions that solve meaningful real-world challenges. This is an opportunity for someone who enjoys balancing research, experimentation, and hands-on product impact in a fast-moving environment where curiosity, creativity, and continuous learning are valued.



QUALIFICATIONS

Education

Ph.D. in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field with a focus on: generative AI, agentic AI, deep learning, reinforcement learning, and/or graph neural networks.

-or-

Equivalent industry experience


Experience:

 - 3-6 years with Applied research in AI.

 - Large language models (LLMs), agentic systems, reinforcement learning, and/or graph neural networks.

 - Successfully shipping AI-powered products (not just publishing or prototyping).


Strengths:

 - Hands-on experience designing and deploying agentic AI systems, including tool usage, function calling, planning, multi-agent orchestration, retrieval-augmented generation, and evaluating agent behavior in production environments.

 - Pre-training and/or fine-tuning foundational large language models.

 - Proficiency in programming languages such as Python or C++, along with expertise in AI frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX).

 - Deep understanding of transformer architectures, deep learning, and generative AI, with experience in natural language processing (NLP) for generative and agentic AI applications.

 - Experience building and deploying scalable AI/ML solutions in real-world applications, including those at national or global scales.

 - Bonus
   
   - Familiarity with supervised fine-tuning, RLHF/DPO, LoRA/PEFT, distillation, and large-scale distributed training.
   
   - AWS certifications related to generative AI.
   
   - Published research findings at top-tier conferences and journals.




WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE

 - Big Data | Research | Innovation | Problem Solving | Experimentation | Productization

 - You thrive in communicative, collaborative, cross-functional environments.

 - Comfortable working at the intersection of research and engineering, able to read papers on Friday and ship a prototype by Monday. Enjoys transforming ambiguous research ideas into reliable production systems.

 - Benefits: stock (RSU) grants, a 401K and generous company match, flexible PTO policy, medical, dental and vision coverage, commuter benefits, in-office healthy snacks, team events

 - Our client is a proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer.

You’ll partner with a diverse team of scientists, engineers, and product leaders to build scalable solutions that solve meaningful real-world challenges. This is an opportunity for someone who enjoys balancing research, experimentation, and hands-on product impact in a fast-moving environment where curiosity, creativity, and continuous learning are valued.

source_jobs.title: Senior Project Manager
source_jobs.description:
TITLE: PROJECT MANAGER / SENIOR PROJECT MANAGER
DEPARTMENT: ENGINEERING – WATER UTILITY INFRASTRUCTURE
LOCATION: AUSTIN, TX
EMPLOYMENT TYPE: FULL-TIME

COMPENSATION: $100K – $145K (COMMENSURATE WITH EXPERIENCE)
+ BONUS + BENEFITS

OVERVIEW

Our client is seeking a Project Manager or Senior Project Manager to lead utility infrastructure projects from planning through delivery. This role supports a wide range of water, wastewater, and stormwater initiatives, with responsibilities scaled based on experience. You will collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, engage directly with clients, and play a key role in delivering high-quality infrastructure solutions that serve communities. Senior-level candidates will take on broader leadership, mentoring, and strategic responsibilities..





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage and deliver utility infrastructure projects, including drinking water, wastewater, and stormwater systems.

 - Develop project scopes, schedules, budgets, and proposals.

 - Coordinate with clients, subconsultants, regulatory agencies, and internal teams.

 - Monitor project performance, including budget, schedule, quality, and risk.

 - Ensure QA/QC compliance for all project deliverables and documentation.

 - Track labor utilization, revenue forecasting, billing, and documentation.

 - Serve as the firm’s representative at client meetings, public hearings, and presentations.

 - More senior PMs will also
   
   - Conduct site visits and construction observations as needed.
   
   - Lead multiple utility infrastructure projects from concept to completion, ensuring schedule, budget, and quality targets are met.
   
   - Mentor engineers and project managers, providing technical guidance and career development support. Oversee proposals, contracts, fee justifications, and budget forecasting.



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Registered Professional Engineer (PE).

 - Proven track record managing drinking water, wastewater, and stormwater projects.

 - Experience range:
   
   - Project Manager: 7+ years of relevant experience
   
   - Senior Project Manager: 10+ years of relevant experience

 - Experience delivering projects on time, within budget, and meeting quality standards.

 - Knowledge of utility design standards, regulations, and construction practices.

 - Excellent communication, leadership, and client management skills.



WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE

 - Take ownership of meaningful infrastructure projects.

 - Collaborate with a dynamic team while gaining mentorship from senior leaders.

 - Competitive benefits and career advancement opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Senior Project Manager - Forensic Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Location: Austin, TX (onsite)
Type: Full-Time
Industry: Forensic Civil Engineering / Infrastructure

Overview:
Join a dynamic, values-driven engineering and architecture firm dedicated to delivering exceptional service to clients and communities. Our client emphasizes respect, professional growth, collaboration, and results. Team members are encouraged to explore innovative solutions, contribute to meaningful projects, and help turn clients’ visions into reality. With competitive benefits, training, and mentorship, this role offers a pathway to a rewarding career beyond just a job.



Position Summary:
We are seeking a Senior Project Manager with experience in forensic engineering or architecture to lead and support a variety of investigative projects. The role involves assessing structural and building enclosure conditions, performing damage and deterioration investigations, and designing repair and rehabilitation solutions for existing facilities. Projects span commercial, residential, industrial, utility infrastructure, and municipal sectors.

This position requires strong technical expertise, excellent communication skills, and the ability to manage multiple assignments while interacting directly with clients and staff. Frequent local and occasional regional travel is required.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Conduct investigations and assessments, preparing accurate technical reports and repair documentation under guidance of senior staff.

 - Manage multiple projects simultaneously and support project scheduling and coordination.

 - Assist in developing repair designs, drawings, and technical specifications.

 - Support senior staff with technical research, report writing, and project execution.

 - Mentor and guide Staff Engineers and Interns as needed.

 - Perform other duties as assigned.



Physical Requirements:

 - Ability to lift or move objects up to 50 lbs.

 - Willingness to climb ladders and scaffolding, access crawl spaces, walk roofs, and work from heights.

 - Comfortable working outdoors in various weather conditions.



Qualifications:

 - Minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree in Civil or Structural Engineering or Architecture from an accredited engineering or architecture school; Other degrees will be considered based on relevant experience

 - Registered Professional Engineer, NCARB Registered Architect, or similar industry licensure/certification

 - 10+ years’ experience

 - AutoCAD and/or Revit experience is a plus

 - Prior design and/or investigation experience preferred

 - Proficient with MS Office applications

 - Valid driver’s license and reliable means of personal transportation


Why Apply:
This role offers the opportunity to work on challenging projects in a collaborative environment, with professional development and mentorship opportunities. If you have a passion for forensic investigations and structural problem-solving, and want to contribute to meaningful projects, this position is for you.

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer (Full-Stack)
source_jobs.description:
Type: Full-Time
Location: USA - Remote

QUALIFICATIONS

Education
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering

-or-

Strengths

WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE

 - Excited to work on identity verification, risk mitigation, due diligence, fraud detection and

 - Benefits: stock (RSU) grants, a 401K and generous company match, flexible PTO policy, medical, dental and vision coverage, commuter benefits, in-office healthy snacks, team events

NOTE: This position is not eligible for immigration or visa sponsorship.

source_jobs.title: Technical Product Manager, Platform and Innovation
source_jobs.description:
Location: Seattle, WA (Hybrid - Downtown Seattle Office)
Compensation: $140,000–$170,000 base salary + RSU equity opportunity + comprehensive benefits




ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

Our client is a publicly traded technology company that develops proprietary software, data intelligence, and advanced analytics solutions used by enterprise organizations to make critical business decisions with greater confidence. Their platform powers identity verification, fraud prevention, risk management, compliance, and customer acquisition initiatives across multiple industries.

As the organization continues to invest heavily in AI-driven innovation, they are seeking a Technical Product Manager, Platform & Innovation to join their product leadership team. This is a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of enterprise software, platform strategy, and applied AI, partnering closely with engineering, data science, and autonomous systems teams to build scalable solutions that support both current products and next-generation capabilities.




THE ROLE

The Technical Product Manager will own the strategy, roadmap, and execution of core platform capabilities that support a broad suite of products and services. This individual will serve as a critical bridge between technical teams and business stakeholders, translating complex requirements into actionable product initiatives across APIs, web applications, mobile solutions, and large-scale data processing systems.

The ideal candidate is a highly technical product leader who enjoys solving complex platform challenges, influencing cross-functional teams, and driving innovation in fast-paced environments.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Define and manage the platform product roadmap, balancing strategic initiatives with business priorities and technical requirements.

 - Partner closely with engineering, data science, and business stakeholders to deliver scalable, high-impact platform capabilities.

 - Lead platform modernization and innovation efforts focused on scalability, reliability, performance, and extensibility.

 - Translate technical requirements into clear product specifications, user stories, and acceptance criteria.

 - Drive API strategy and evolution, ensuring a seamless developer experience and robust integration capabilities.

 - Collaborate with executive leadership, product teams, and customer-facing stakeholders to align platform investments with company objectives.

 - Operate within Agile and Kanban environments, participating in planning, backlog management, and delivery processes.

 - Identify technical risks and proactively develop mitigation strategies related to scalability, system availability, and integrations.

 - Support sales, onboarding, and customer success teams by providing platform expertise and product guidance.

 - Evaluate emerging technologies, market trends, and competitive developments to inform future platform strategy.

 - Partner with AI and autonomous systems teams to create product requirements that support agent-driven engineering workflows and AI-enabled delivery models.



QUALIFICATIONS

Required

 - 5–8 years of product management experience with a strong emphasis on technical, platform, or infrastructure-focused products.

 - Demonstrated success owning platform roadmaps and delivering complex technical initiatives.

 - Strong understanding of software development lifecycles, system architecture, and scalable platform design.

 - Experience working with REST APIs, integrations, and developer tools such as Postman, Swagger/OpenAPI, or similar technologies.

 - Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate between technical and non-technical audiences.

 - Proven ability to prioritize competing initiatives and make informed decisions in fast-moving environments.

 - Experience supporting web, mobile, and large-scale data processing solutions.

 - Strong cross-functional collaboration skills with engineering, product, data, and business

COMPENSATION: $100K – $110K (COMMENSURATE WITH EXPERIENCE)
+ BONUS + BENEFITS
OVERVIEW

Our client is seeking a motivated Project Manager to oversee utility infrastructure projects from planning through delivery. This role balances technical oversight, client coordination, and project execution, with opportunities for professional growth and mentorship under senior leadership.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage utility infrastructure projects including drinking water, wastewater, and stormwater systems.

 - Develop project scopes, schedules, budgets, and proposals.

 - Coordinate with clients, subconsultants, regulatory agencies, and internal teams.

 - Supervise project staff and mentor engineers/technicians under senior guidance.

 - Perform QA/QC reviews, address design issues, and assist with construction-related problems.

 - Track labor utilization, revenue forecasting, billing, and documentation.

 - Conduct occasional site visits and construction observations.



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Registered Professional Engineer (PE).

 - 7+ years of experience in utility infrastructure engineering.

 - Proficiency in project management and client communication.

 - Experience delivering projects on time, within budget, and meeting quality standards.

 - Knowledge of utility design standards, regulations, and construction practices.



WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE

 - Take ownership of meaningful infrastructure projects.

 - Collaborate with a dynamic team while gaining mentorship from senior leaders.

 - Competitive benefits and career advancement opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Project Manager
source_jobs.description:
TITLE: PROJECT MANAGER
DEPARTMENT: ENGINEERING – WATER UTILITY INFRASTRUCTURE
LOCATION: TAMPA, FL
EMPLOYMENT TYPE: FULL-TIME



COMPENSATION: $100K – $110K (COMMENSURATE WITH EXPERIENCE)
+ BONUS + BENEFITS
OVERVIEW

Our client is seeking a motivated Project Manager to oversee utility infrastructure projects from planning through delivery. This role balances technical oversight, client coordination, and project execution, with opportunities for professional growth and mentorship under senior leadership.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage utility infrastructure projects including drinking water, wastewater, and stormwater systems.

 - Develop project scopes, schedules, budgets, and proposals.

 - Coordinate with clients, subconsultants, regulatory agencies, and internal teams.

 - Supervise project staff and mentor engineers/technicians under senior guidance.

 - Perform QA/QC reviews, address design issues, and assist with construction-related problems.

 - Track labor utilization, revenue forecasting, billing, and documentation.

 - Conduct occasional site visits and construction observations.



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Registered Professional Engineer (PE).

 - 7+ years of experience in utility infrastructure engineering.

 - Proficiency in project management and client communication.

 - Experience delivering projects on time, within budget, and meeting quality standards.

 - Knowledge of utility design standards, regulations, and construction practices.



WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE

 - Take ownership of meaningful infrastructure projects.

 - Collaborate with a dynamic team while gaining mentorship from senior leaders.

 - Competitive benefits and career advancement opportunities.

notes: Mostly project management and engineering roles, with some IT and software positions. No explicit mention of sponsorship or clearance requirements.
